title: Develop the prior blend idea into an AUC-preserving specialist rank ensemble
summary: For ROC AUC, monotone calibration usually does not help ranking, and scikit-learn notes isotonic can even alter AUC by introducing ties.
rationale: For ROC AUC, monotone calibration usually does not help ranking, and scikit-learn notes isotonic can even alter AUC by introducing ties. That suggests the blend should optimize rank diversity across experts rather than chase probability calibration alone.
implementation_hint: Train a few intentionally different experts, for example a raw-categorical CatBoost specialist, a numeric/meta-feature booster, a wet-weather expert and a late-race expert. Produce strict out-of-fold predictions on race-held-out splits, convert each expert to fold-wise ranks or sigmoid-calibrated logits, then fit a very small linear blend on OOF AUC. Avoid isotonic in the final rank path.
expected_effect: Modest leaderboard lift if experts disagree mainly around pit-window boundaries where ranking is uncertain.
risk: If experts are too correlated, the blend adds noise; compare against plain rank-average and keep stacking fully out-of-fold.
